data_sources.title: Data Sources
data_sources.subtitle: Explore where Blooma’s activity guidance comes from

content.hero_subtitle: Understanding where prenatal activity guidance comes from

intro_blocks.label: WHY SOURCES MATTER
intro_blocks.heading: Building Guidance on Trusted References
paragraphs: This section explains where Blooma’s exercise, yoga, and walking recommendations come from, how those sources are organized, and how they support safer pregnancy wellness content.
Blooma uses source-linked datasets and trusted reference materials so prenatal activity guidance feels more transparent, structured, and reliable.
The goal is to help users understand that the app’s wellness recommendations are supported by meaningful documentation rather than presented as isolated content.

items.title: Why Sources Matter
items.description: Prenatal guidance should be based on trusted medical and research-backed references.

items.title: How Blooma Uses Them
items.description: Sources support activity planning, safety guidance, and condition-aware recommendations.

content.hero_subtitle: Built for clarity, traceability, and prenatal relevance

intro_blocks.label: SOURCE PRINCIPLES
intro_blocks.heading: Designed for Reliable Wellness Content
paragraphs: Blooma’s source system is structured to support transparency and consistency across exercise, yoga, and walking guidance.
Each source is selected to support prenatal safety, activity relevance, and a clearer documentation process inside the app.

items.title: Source Transparency
items.description: Each activity should connect back to a trusted source.

items.title: Medical Relevance
items.description: Sources are chosen for prenatal safety and wellness planning.

items.title: Activity-Specific Mapping
items.description: Exercise, yoga, and walking follow separate but connected source models.

items.title: Condition-Aware Support
items.description: Sources may support both general and condition-specific guidance.

items.title: Documentation-Ready
items.description: The structure supports app use, review, and future updates.

content.hero_subtitle: Three main collections support Blooma’s wellness guidance

intro_blocks.label: DATASET COLLECTIONS
intro_blocks.heading: Exercise, Yoga, and Walking
paragraphs: Blooma’s data source layer is organized around three main prenatal activity collections: exercise, yoga, and walking.
Each dataset has its own structure based on the type of activity it supports, while still fitting into a consistent source and documentation system.
These collections help the app organize recommendations, trimester alignment, and activity-specific educational content.

items.title: Exercise Dataset
items.description: Supports prenatal exercise planning with duration, intensity, trimester, and source-linked guidance.

items.title: Yoga Dataset
items.description: Provides structured prenatal yoga content with source-backed movement and wellness context.

items.title: Walking Dataset
items.description: Supports walking guidance with duration, frequency, distance, and progression-based recommendations.

content.hero_subtitle: A balanced mix of medical, research, and educational sources

intro_blocks.label: REFERENCE TYPES
intro_blocks.heading: More Than One Kind of Source
paragraphs: Blooma uses a mix of professional guidelines, public health recommendations, clinical education, research literature, and selected books.
This helps the app support both broad prenatal safety understanding and activity-specific guidance in a more complete way.

items.title: Professional Guidelines
items.description: Recognized recommendations from health and medical bodies.

items.title: Clinical Health Education
items.description: Patient-friendly guidance from trusted medical institutions.

items.title: Research Literature
items.description: Studies and indexed articles that support movement and safety understanding.

items.title: Books & Foundational Texts
items.description: Selected wellness and yoga references used where relevant.

intro_blocks.label: SOURCE ORGANIZATIONS
intro_blocks.heading: Trusted Names in the Source Layer
paragraphs: Blooma includes references from recognized medical organizations, public health institutions, and clinical education providers.
These organizations strengthen the credibility and structure of prenatal wellness guidance across the app.

items.title: ACOG
items.description: Supports pregnancy-safe exercise guidance and prenatal activity recommendations.

items.title: WHO
items.description: Provides broad physical activity guidance relevant to pregnancy-safe movement planning.

items.title: NHS
items.description: Offers pregnancy exercise guidance in clear and user-friendly language.

items.title: Mayo Clinic
items.description: Supports patient-facing pregnancy and exercise education.

content.hero_subtitle: Sources are organized to support different prenatal needs

intro_blocks.label: CONDITION COVERAGE
intro_blocks.heading: Supporting General and Condition-Specific Guidance
paragraphs: Blooma’s source collections support both general prenatal wellness guidance and selected condition-aware activity planning.
This helps the app organize content more thoughtfully for users who may need gentler pacing, more careful activity choices, or additional safety context.
Condition coverage may vary slightly across the datasets, depending on how each activity collection was prepared.

items.title: General Prenatal
items.description: Core wellness support across exercise, yoga, and walking.

items.title: Hypertension
items.description: Supports more cautious activity planning where needed.

items.title: Thyroid
items.description: Helps support adaptable guidance for variable energy and tolerance.

items.title: Obesity
items.description: Supports lower-impact and comfort-aware movement planning.

items.title: Anemia
items.description: Supports gentler and fatigue-aware activity structure.

items.title: Diabetes
items.description: Included in the exercise dataset for more targeted support.

content.hero_subtitle: Turning references into structured prenatal guidance

intro_blocks.label: CONTENT FLOW
intro_blocks.heading: How Source Data Becomes App Content
paragraphs: Blooma does not simply display source material as raw references. Instead, source-linked activity records are organized into app-friendly recommendations, educational sections, and safety-aware guidance.
This helps connect documentation, activity planning, and user-facing clarity in one consistent experience.

items.title: Collect Source-Linked Entries
items.description: Each activity record is stored with its source and key supporting details.

items.title: Organize by Activity Type
items.description: Exercise, yoga, and walking are maintained as separate collections.

items.title: Map to Trimester & Safety
items.description: Activities are aligned with pregnancy stage and safer movement structure.

items.title: Support Adaptation
items.description: Condition-based entries help guide safer alternatives where available.

items.title: Create User-Friendly Content
items.description: Source records are transformed into clear and supportive app content.

intro_blocks.label: QUALITY FRAMEWORK
intro_blocks.heading: What Makes a Source Useful
paragraphs: Blooma’s source layer is designed to reflect quality, pregnancy relevance, and practical usability inside the app.
Strong sources help support safer movement planning, clearer documentation, and more reassuring educational content.

items.title: Recognized Authority
items.description: Preference is given to respected medical and public health sources.

items.title: Pregnancy Relevance
items.description: Sources should clearly support prenatal wellness context.

items.title: Safety Usefulness
items.description: Sources should help inform safer movement decisions.

items.title: Structured Metadata
items.description: Whenever possible, source details include author or publication context.

items.title: Documentation Continuity
items.description: Sources should support future updates and long-term maintenance.

content.hero_subtitle: Source visibility supports trust, not diagnosis or treatment

intro_blocks.label: IMPORTANT DISCLAIMER
intro_blocks.heading: Understanding the Role of Sources
paragraphs: Blooma’s Data Sources section is intended to improve transparency around the educational and activity content used in the app.
Source inclusion does not by itself guarantee suitability for every user and does not replace professional medical advice.
Users should consult a qualified healthcare provider for personalized pregnancy care and activity decisions.

items.title: Transparency Purpose
items.description: This section explains where guidance comes from.

items.title: Consult Professionals
items.description: Always seek medical advice for personal care decisions.
